title: Abdominal Distension - Small Bowel Obstruction
chief_complaint: Abdominal pain, distension, bilious vomiting, and constipation for 3 days. Previous abdominal surgery for hysterectomy.

physical_exam.abdomen: Distended, tympanic, hyperactive bowel sounds (tinkling), diffuse tenderness, no rebound, surgical scar

hidden_data.ct_abdomen: Dilated small bowel loops with air-fluid levels, transition point at mid-ileum due to adhesive band, no ischemia

correct_diagnosis: Small Bowel Obstruction due to Adhesions
key_learning_points: Adhesions = most common cause of SBO
CT with contrast shows transition point
Surgery if strangulation suspected or fails conservative

patient_responses.pain: It's crampy and comes in waves
patient_responses.flatus: I haven't passed gas in 3 days

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed! SBO from adhesions in a patient with prior surgery.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is SBO. The prior surgery + CT findings confirm it.
feedback.attending_note: SBO: NPO, NGT decompression, IV fluids, serial abdominal exams. Surgery if signs of strangulation (fever, leukocytosis, peritonitis) or failure of conservative management after 48-72h.
